market_summary: Memorial Day holiday — US equity and bond markets CLOSED Monday May 25. Thin liquidity across all dollar-denominated assets. Crypto trading 24/7 with holiday-thin orderbooks. Asia rallied hard: Nikkei +2.87% to 65,158, Taiwan Taiex +3.26%, CSI 300 +1.58% — strong risk-on tone on US-Iran deal optimism. Gold up ~1% intraday to $4,561-4,565 on geopolitical hedging despite risk-on mood. BTC up +1.68% to ~$77,215 (Crypto.com CFD). WTI oil crashed ~6% to $91/bbl on Iran deal hopes — massive move. Silver recovering toward $78 after last week's flat close. Putin visited Beijing May 19-20 (40+ cooperation agreements with Xi). Fed Chair Warsh took oath. Market consensus: rates at 3.50-3.75% hold, no cuts 2026, possible hike Jan 2027. Every US-Iran headline is moving markets with amplified holiday-liquidity impact.
key_events_this_week: Monday May 25: US CLOSED (Memorial Day). NYSE, NASDAQ, bond markets all shut. Crypto + metals futures only.
Tuesday May 26: US markets reopen. Consumer Confidence (10:00 ET). Dallas Fed Manufacturing Survey (10:30 ET). Philadelphia Fed Non-Manufacturing (08:30 ET). SCE Public Policy Survey (11:00 ET).
Wednesday May 27: New Residential Sales (10:00 ET). Richmond Fed Manufacturing Survey (10:00 ET). Dallas Fed Texas Retail Outlook (10:30 ET). Corporate Bond Market Distress Index (10:00 ET).

Thursday May 28 — HEAVY DATA DAY: GDP Q1 2nd Estimate (08:30 ET), PCE Deflator + Core PCE (08:30 ET), Durable Goods Orders (08:30 ET), Initial Claims (08:30 ET). Core PCE last at 3.2% YoY — Fed's preferred inflation gauge. THIS IS THE WEEK'S MAIN EVENT.
Friday May 29: Chicago PMI. Multivariate Core Trend Inflation (10:00 ET). NY Fed Staff Nowcast (12:45 ET). Shangri-La Dialogue opens (defense summit, potential Asia geopolitical headlines).
NO FOMC this week (next: Jun 16-17). NO CPI (next: Jun 10). NO NFP (next: Jun 5).
Ongoing: US-Iran peace deal negotiations — binary outcome risk dominating all asset classes.
overnight_risks: Iran deal binary: Trump said 50/50 odds. Deal = Hormuz reopens = oil -$10-15 further, gold dips on reduced haven bid, equities gap up Tuesday, risk-on crypto rally. No deal = oil rebound to $97+, gold $4,600+, equities gap down.
Oil already moved: WTI crashed 6% to $91 on Iran deal hopes — if talks collapse, violent reversal. If deal materializes, WTI could test $80s.
Memorial Day thin liquidity persists through Monday evening. CME equity/rate futures halt 12:00 CT, reopen 5:00 PM CT for Tuesday session.
Asia risk-on momentum (Nikkei +2.87%) could carry into European open, setting positive tone for Tuesday US reopen.
MSTR gap risk: BTC weekend move ($77.2K) directly sets Tuesday MSTR gap. MSTR closed $159.89 Friday — BTC strength implies gap up.
Bond market stress: 30Y at 5.06-5.10%. New Fed Chair Warsh taking oath — any policy signals during transition amplified.
Israel-Lebanon escalation: Israeli airstrikes on 15+ southern Lebanon villages ongoing. Complicates Iran diplomacy.
Thursday PCE + GDP combo: If core PCE >3.2%, rate hike pricing intensifies, risk assets sell off. If <3.0%, relief rally.

instrument_news.XAU-USD: Gold up ~1% intraday to $4,561-4,565 on May 25. Trading within $4,441-$4,577 range. Central banks bought 244t Q1 (+3% YoY), ~190t/quarter pace expected for 2026. 95% of central banks expect global gold reserves to rise in 2026. Gold ETPs hold 4,000+ tonnes globally (+25% in 2025, $650B AUM). US-listed gold ETFs saw $830M inflows in April, reducing YTD outflows to $1.5B. J.P. Morgan targets $5,000 by Q4 2026. Ascending channel intact — upside target $4,745. Support $4,410-4,441, resistance $4,577-4,630. Iran deal remains binary catalyst: deal = haven unwind dip, no deal = $4,800+.
instrument_news.XAG-USD: Silver recovering toward $78, after holding $76 on Friday. Gold/silver ratio compressed from 80:1 (late 2025) to below 55:1 after May 10-11 US-China tariff truce — one of fastest compressions in years. At 55:1 silver cheap relative to gold. 6th consecutive supply deficit year (762M oz cumulative drawdowns). Bank of America bold range: $135-309 year-end (ratio compression scenarios). UBS revised down to $80. J.P. Morgan avg $81/oz for 2026. Goldman $85-100 range. Support $73-75 critical, resistance $78-83. Structural case strong: physical market tighter than most realize.
instrument_news.BTC-USD: BTC at ~$77,215, up +1.68% on Sunday. Trading in $75K-$80K range. Key resistance: $80,000 psychological, $82,000-$82,500 (200 SMA/EMA confluence), $83K-$85K major barrier. Key support: $75K, $73K, $72K (100-day MA). Broke out of multi-month descending channel — bullish signal. BTC ETF flows: April strong ($2.44B net), but May saw $1B outflows May 11-15 plus record $635M single-day outflow May 13. On-chain: MVRV ~2.3x (measured, not euphoric), SOPR 1.03 (slight profit-taking), exchange reserves at 7yr lows. SpaceX disclosed $1.29B BTC stash (May 22). Strategic Bitcoin Reserve bill pushed by lawmakers (May 21). SEC delayed tokenized stock plan (May 23). CLARITY Act regulatory framework shaping up as central narrative.
instrument_news.ETH-USD: Ethereum at ~$2,128, neutral-to-bearish. RSI 36 — approaching oversold. ETH ETFs: lost $36.3M in April, further $255M outflows May 11-15. Altcoin ETFs (XRP $60.5M, SOL $58.1M) absorbing flows that might have gone to ETH. Resistance $2,150-2,170 key zone to reclaim; support $2,085-2,100, then $2,043 and $2,000 psychological. Rising Treasury yields creating immediate downward pressure. BTC dominance at 60% — Bitcoin Season, ETH underperforming. Glamsterdam upgrade June 2026 remains catalyst.
instrument_news.MSTR: Strategy (MSTR) closed Friday at $159.89, with another source citing $164.63. 52-week range $104.17-$457.22, down ~64% from ATH. Resistance at $163.68, $169.46, then $206.80. Support at $146.44, $137.65, then $106.99. Saylor signaled willingness to sell BTC — policy shift from 'never sell'. Company holds 843,738 BTC. US markets closed Monday — Tuesday gap will be set by BTC weekend action (~$77.2K, up from Friday). Technical: sell signal issued May 11, down -18.4% since. RSI 72.3 (overbought on one reading), MACD bullish. Mixed signals.

metals_outlook: Structural bull intact. Central bank buying accelerated (244t Q1, 95% of CBs expect reserves to rise). Gold ETPs above 4,000t globally. Gold at $4,561 up 1% intraday on geopolitical hedging even as risk-on mood prevails — dual driver (haven + inflation hedge). Silver recovering toward $78 with gold/silver ratio at ~55:1 (compressed from 80:1 late 2025 — already repriced significantly). Silver supply deficit in 6th consecutive year with 762M oz cumulative drawdowns — physical market structurally tight. Iran deal remains the near-term binary: deal = metals dip as geopolitical premium unwinds + oil collapse reduces inflation fears; no deal = gold $4,800+, silver $80+. Thursday PCE data is the fundamental catalyst — core PCE >3.2% = inflation hedge bid strengthens, <3.0% = rate cut hopes revive (also gold-positive via real rates). Either way, structural case supports metals. 30Y yields >5% remain persistent headwind via real rates.
oil_outlook: MASSIVE MOVE: WTI crashed ~6% to $91/bbl on May 25 as Iran deal optimism surged. Brent averaging ~$106 in May-June per EIA. This is THE binary trade. Strait of Hormuz disrupted since Feb 28 — 14+ mb/d shut in, unprecedented supply shock. Global inventories falling 8.5M b/d in Q2. Observed inventories drew 129mb in March, 117mb in April. UAE departed OPEC May 1 — frees 1.6M bpd shut-in capacity once Hormuz reopens. Oil prices up 40%+ from pre-war levels despite today's selloff. Deal success = further decline to $80s as Hormuz reopens + UAE ramps outside OPEC quotas. Deal failure = violent rebound to $97+ and potential $110-114 retest. Qatar LNG exports still -17% from Ras Laffan attack. Every Trump/Rubio tweet on Iran can swing oil $3-5 in thin liquidity.
crypto_outlook: Cautiously constructive. BTC at $77.2K up 1.68%, breaking out of descending channel (bullish). But Crypto Fear & Greed at 39 (Fear) — sentiment deteriorated from 61 last week. ETF flow picture mixed: strong April ($2.44B), weak mid-May ($1B outflows). On-chain supply squeeze building: exchange reserves 7yr low, MVRV 2.3x (measured, not euphoric), SOPR 1.03. Key catalysts: SpaceX $1.29B BTC disclosure (institutional legitimacy), Strategic BTC Reserve bill (demand narrative), but SEC tokenized stock delay (regulatory uncertainty). ETH at $2,128 struggling — RSI 36 approaching oversold, ETF outflows continuing, altcoin ETFs absorbing capital. BTC dominance 60% = Bitcoin Season. Near-term: $80K is the breakout level — daily close above opens $84-85.5K. Memorial Day thin liquidity = outsized moves possible. CLARITY Act regulatory framework could be medium-term catalyst.
sentiment_shift: Divergent but converging on risk-on. Asia rallied hard (Nikkei +2.87%, Taiex +3.26%) pricing in Iran deal success. Oil crashed 6% confirming risk-on rotation. Gold up 1% — unusual for risk-on, suggests structural demand beyond pure haven. Equity Fear & Greed ~59 (Greed) vs Crypto Fear & Greed 39 (Fear) — persistent divergence, but BTC +1.68% today suggests crypto catching up. Iran deal will force convergence: either broad risk-on (deal) or risk-off (failure). Bond market skeptical — 30Y >5%, Warsh taking oath. New Fed Chair transition adds policy uncertainty. Thursday May 28 PCE + GDP combo is the fundamental reset point. Market consensus: no rate cuts 2026, possible Jan 2027 hike. CPI 3.8% April keeping hawkish pressure. Memorial Day thin liquidity amplifies all moves through Tuesday open.

treasury_yields.2Y: 4.14% — above Fed funds rate (3.50-3.75%), market pricing no near-term cuts
treasury_yields.30Y: 5.06-5.10% — near highest since 2007, significant headwind for gold via real yields

dxy: ~99.3 — weaker dollar tone as Iran deal hopes reduce safe-haven USD demand. Modest tailwind for metals/crypto if continues.
fed: 3.50-3.75% hold. New Chair Warsh sworn in. Market fully pricing 25bp hike Jan 2027. CPI 3.8% April, Core PCE 3.2% — inflation sticky. Next FOMC Jun 16-17. Stagflation backdrop: mixed labor, inflation above target from oil shocks, geopolitical risks.

BTC-USD.notes: 200 SMA/EMA confluence at 82000-82500. Break above 80K opens 84-85.5K. Below 75K targets 73K then 72K (100d MA).

ETH-USD.notes: RSI 36 near oversold. Must reclaim 2150-2170 for recovery. Below 2085 targets 2043 then 2000.

XAG-USD.notes: Gold/silver ratio at ~55:1 (compressed). Recovering toward $78. Support $73-75 critical. Supply deficit structural.

XAU-USD.notes: Ascending channel. Upside target 4745. Above 4577 targets 4610-4630. Below 4441 risk of breakdown.

MSTR.notes: Closed $159.89 Fri. Gap up likely Tue if BTC holds $77K+. Sell signal since May 11. Next major resistance $206.80.
